rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Why is it considered better to grow a rose from cutting rather than from seeds?
rows.answer:
To grow plants by cutting rather than from seeds is a method of artificial asexual reproduction. It is considered better to grow a rose from cutting rather than from seeds due to following reasons:
(i) It is the only method of reproduction in seedless plants.
(ii) We get a fully matured plant in a very short time.
(iii) It is a short cut method for rapid multiplication.
(iv) It helps to preserve the type of character that a plant breeder desires.
(v) It is a very easy and economical method for the multiplication of ornamental plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q2
rows.question: Why does bread mould grow profusely on a moist slice of bread rather than on a dry slice of bread?
rows.answer: Hyphae for digestion of bread nutrients secrete enzymes. For their working and absorption of digested food, hyphae require moisture. Moistened bread slice offers both moisture and nutrients to the bread mould, hence, it grows profusely. Dry slice of bread offers nutrients but not moisture hence, hyphae fail to grow.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Why fertilisation cannot take place in flowers if pollination does not occur?
rows.answer:
In a flower, fertilisation requires both male and female gametes.
If pollination does not occur, male gamete is not available, hence, fertilisation would take place.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Why do mouse parents produce mouse-like offspring ? Give reasons.
rows.answer: Mouse parents produce mouse-like offspring because their reproductive cells contain chromosomes with correctly coded nucleic acid, i.e., reproduction is specific.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Why does an exposed moist piece of bread get covered with a cottony growth?
rows.answer: The moist piece of bread is an ideal substratum for growth of Rhizopus or bread mould. The spores of this fungus are nearly always present in the air. Some of them settle over the bread, germinate and form mycelia which appear as cottony growths. Later on a number of black spots develop. They represent sporangia.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q6
rows.question: Why do green grass plants spring up in dry fields on their own after the rains?
rows.answer: The fields have dry stems of the old grass plants all over. These dry stems have buds in an inactive state. By getting rainwater, the bud present on the dry grass stem gets activated and grow to produce new grass plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q7
rows.question: Why are budding, fragmentation and regeneration all considered as asexual types of reproduction. With neat diagrams explain the process of regeneration in Planaria.
rows.answer:
Budding, fragmentation and regeneration are considered as asexual types of reproduction because:
(i) They involve only one parent,
(ii) Gametes are not involved in reproduction.
(iii) Meiotic division does not take place at any stage of this type of reproduction.
Fig. Regeneration in Planaria

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q8
rows.question: Why is it an advantage for the testes to be situated in the scrotal sac outside the main body cavity? Can you think of one disadvantage?
rows.answer: The testes to be situated in the scrotal sac outside the main body cavity because the formation of sperms require a low temperature than the normal body temperature. Its disadvantage of being outside the body cavity is that it is more prone to injury.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q2
rows.question: A piece of potato tuber with an eye produces a new plant while another piece without an eye does not. Give reason.
rows.answer: A piece of potato tuber having an eye possesses bud which sprouts to form a new plant. Piece of tuber without an eye does not form a new plant due to absence of bud.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q3
rows.question: A man went to a doctor and the doctor removed his Cowper's gland. How would the removal of cowper's gland affect the man?
rows.answer: Cowper's gland secrete an alkaline fluid which helps to neutralise the acidic environment of the male urethra and female reproductive tract. Removal of cowper's gland thus would inactivate and kill sperms in male urethra and female reproductive tract.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q4
rows.question: If you have to go in a village during AIDS prevention programme, what points will you explain to the villagers?
rows.answer:
A few points for prevention of AIDS are:
(i) Use condoms while having sex.
(ii) Pregnant ladies should be checked.
(iii) Use disposable syringe.
(iv) Blood transfusion should be done carefully.
# Case Study Based MCQs (CBSE Sample Issued 2021) Male Reproduction System

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Why is variation beneficial to the species but not necessarily for the individual ?
rows.answer: Variations are differences among individuals produced as a result of errors caused during DNA copying at the time of cell division. If a reproducing organism is suitable to a particular niche and niche drastically changed then the population could be wiped out and got adversely affected. The variants may have the ability to survive under adverse condition. The surviving organism may reproduce and develop a kind of population which is suited for their niche. This shows that the species is able to survive but the individual lacking suitable features failed to do so. Thus, variation is beneficial to the species but not necessarily for the individual.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q2
rows.question: How will an organism be benefitted if it reproduces through spores?
rows.answer:
Spore formation is a method of asexual reproduction which is found in non - flowering plants like fungi (Rhizopus) and bacteria, in which the parent plant produces hundreds of tiny spores which turn into a new plant.
An organism can be benefitted in following ways if it produces through spores:
(i) Spores are thick walled which protect the organism from unfavourable condition and remain dormant till favourable conditions become available.
(ii) Since it is a form of asexual reproduction, so the new organism won't lose its parental characters.
(iii) At a time, several numbers of spores are produced so cannot extinct very easily. Hence, numerous plants can be grown and does not require any medium for dispersal.
(iv) Spores are light weighted so they can be easily transfered from one plant to other for germination.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Can you think of reasons why more complex organisms cannot give rise to new individuals through regeneration ?
rows.answer:
Regeneration is the process carried out by specialised cells which involves the formation of new individual from its body parts. These cells proliferate and make large numbers of cells. From this mass of cells, different
cells undergo changes to become various cell types and tissues. Since the simple organisms comprise of only one type of cells which assist that organism to grow and develop in Hydra and Planaria.
Complex organism have organ - system level of organisation in which all the organs of organ system work as an interconnected unit. They can regenerate their body parts such as skin, muscles etc. but cannot give rise to new individual through regeneration.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Why is vegetative propagation practised for growing some types of plants?
rows.answer:
Vegetative propagation is practiced for growing certain types of plants because:
(i) It is the only method of reproduction in those plants which have lost their capacity to produce seeds like banana, orange, rose, jasmine etc.
(ii) Plants raised by vegetative propagation can bear flowers and fruits earlier than those produced from seeds.
(iii) Plants produced by this method are genetically similar enough to the parent plant to have all its characteristics.
(iv) Vegetative propagation is used in methods such as layering or grafting to grow many plants like sugarcane, roses, or grapes for agricultural purposes.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Why is DNA copying an essential part of the process of reproduction?
rows.answer: DNA copying is the biological process of producing two identical copies of DNA from one original DNA molecule. DNA are the carriers of genetic information which transfers the information or characters from parents to offspring. It also helps to generates variations during sexual reproduction and leads to evolution. This explains how DNA copying is an essential part of the process of replication.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q6
rows.question: How is the process of pollination different from fertilization?
rows.answer:
Difference between pollination and fertilisation are as follows-
| S.No. | Pollination | Fertilisation |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (i) | Pollination is the process of transferring pollen grains from the male anther to female stigma. | Fertilisation is fusion of male and female gametes to form a new organism of the same species. |
| (ii) | It occurs in the early stages of sexual reproduction in all flowering plants. | It is preceded by the pollination in all flowering plants. |
| (iii) | It requires external factors like wind, birds, insects, animals etc. | It does not require any external factor and can be achieved by the growth of the pollen tube. |
| (iv) | In pollination, there is no formation of pollen tube occur. | In fertilisation, pollen tube is formed for transferring male gametes into an egg cell. |
| (v) | Pollination occurs only in plants. | Fertilisation occurs in almost in every living organism like plants, humans, animals, insects etc. |

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q7
rows.question: What is the role of the seminal vesicles and the prostate gland?
rows.answer:
Seminal vesicles are one pair which lies at the base of urinary bladder behind its neck. They secrete a viscous fluid for nourishment of sperms in human male.
Prostate Gland : It is single in number which surrounds the first part of urethra. Its secretion is poured into urethra by many ducts.
It secretes alkaline fluid which is discharged into urethra and neutralize any acidity caused due to residual urine. Both the parts add their secreations so that the sperms mix with the fluid which makes their transport easier.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q8
rows.question: What are the changes seen in girls at the time of puberty ?
rows.answer:
The changes seen in girls at the time of puberty are as follows:
- Pelvic region and the overall fat content of the body increases.
- The waist narrows due to more deposition of fat on the hips under the influence of hormones.
- Mammary gland starts developing with dark nipples at the tip of breast.
- Pubic hairs appear in the genital area, underarms, face, hands, and legs.
- At the time of puberty, menstrual cycle begins.
- The size of uterus and ovary increases.
- More secretion of oil from the skin, which results in the appearance of pimples.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q9
rows.question: If a woman is using a copper-T, will it help in protecting her from sexually transmitted diseases?
rows.answer:
- No, copper-T will not help a woman to protect her from sexually transmitted disease.
- The copper-T is a simple intra-uterine device (IUD) made of a flexible, "T" shaped piece of plastic wrapped with a thin copper containing wire. It only prevents the implantation of the embryo in the uterus. As copper- T does not prevent entry of semen so it is not able to protect a woman from sexually transmitted diseases (STDs).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q1

Which of the following is not a part of the female reproductive system in human beings?
(a) Ovary
(b) Uterus
(c) Vas deferens
(d) Fallopian tube
rows.answer: (c) Ovary, uterus and fallopian tube are the part of female reproductive system but vas deferens is a part of male reproductive system in human beings. The sperms are delivered through the vas deferens which unites with a tube coming from the urinary bladder.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q2

(a) sepals
(b) ovules
(c) carpel
(d) pollen grains
rows.answer: (d) Stamens are the male reproductive parts of flowers. A stamen consists of an anther (which produces pollen) and a filament.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q3
rows.question: Why does menstruation occur ?
rows.answer: Menstruation, or period, is normal vaginal bleeding that occurs as part of a woman's monthly cycle. Every month, one egg is released by an ovary and if the egg is not fertilised, it lives for about one day. Since the ovary releases one egg every month, the uterus also prepares itself every month to receive a fertilised egg. Thus its lining becomes thick and spongy. The main purpose of this lining is to provide nourishment to the embryo. Now, however, this lining is not needed any longer. So, the lining slowly breaks and comes out through the vagina as blood and mucous which causes menstruation.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q5
rows.question: How does reproduction help in providing stability to populations of species?
rows.answer:
Reproduction is a life process which enables an organism to reproduce its own offspring. Thus, they continue their species without extinction. Reproduction helps in providing stability to populations of species by considering following points:
- It provides stability to population of species by giving birth to new individuals as the rate of birth must be at par with the rate of death to provide stability to population of a species.
- The consistency of DNA copying during reproduction is also important for the maintenance of body design features that allow the organism to use that particular niche. Therefore, reproduction is linked to the stability of populations of species.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q6
rows.question: What are the functions performed by the testes in human beings?
rows.answer:
Testes is a part of both male reproductive system and endocrine system. They are located behind the penis in a pouch of skin called the scrotum. The functions performed by the testes in human beings are as follows:
- It helps in the production of sperms in male which takes part in sexual reproduction.
- It assists in the secretion of the testosterone hormone, which plays a key role in the development of secondary sexual characters in male.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q7
rows.question: How are the modes of reproduction different in unicellular and multicellular organisms?
rows.answer:
Unicellular organisms are single celled organisms in which all the physiological activities like reproduction, respiration excretion etc. are performed by a single cell. Multicellular organisms are those which consist of more than one cell, in contrast to unicellular organisms. Therefore, modes of reproduction are different in unicellular and multicellular organism as:
- Unicellular organisms always reproduce by asexual methods like budding, regeneration, spore formation, binary fission, multiple fission, etc. Multicellular organism, on the other hand, uses both sexual and asexual method for reproduction.
- In unicellular organism, there is only single cell which perform all the activities so there is no specialised cells and tissues that are meant for reproduction. But in multicellular organism, they have well developed reproductive organ which takes part in reproduction.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q8
rows.question: What could be the reasons for adopting contraceptive methods?
rows.answer:
Contraceptive method is any method, medicine, or device used to prevent pregnancy. It includes mechanical barrier method, hormonal method, surgical method, IUDs, etc.
The reasons for adopting contraceptive methods are as follows:
D
E
(i) Population control - It helps in controlling the population rate or birth rate.
(ii) Prevents unwanted pregnancies - It helps to maintain a women's health by preventing unwanted pregnancies and by keeping gap between consecutive pregnancies.
(iii) Prevents from STDs - There are few contraceptive methods like condoms which prevents spread of sexually transmitted diseases and lethal diseases like HIV/AIDS.
(iv) It assists in family planning - Family planning/ contraception reduces the need for abortion, especially unsafe abortion.

(b) Statement (A) is correct but (B) is incorrect.

rows.answer: (d) The phenomenon in which female gamete develops into embryo without getting fused with male gamete (fertilisation) is called parthenogenesis. Autogamy is a form of self-fertilisation in which fission of the cell nucleus occurs without division of the cell, the two pronuclei so formed reuniting to form the synkaryon. Parthenocarpy is the production of fruit without fertilisation of an egg in the ovary. Synkaryon is a nucleus formed by the fusion of two pre-existing nuclei.

rows.answer: (c) In double fertilization, one male gamete fuses with the egg and results in the formation of a diploid zygote and this process is called fertilization. The other male gamete fuses with the two polar nuclei to form a triploid endosperm. This process is called triple fusion. After fertilisation, the fertilized ovule forms the seed while the tissues of the ovary become the fruit.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q30

[NTSE]

(c) Oviduct
(d) Placenta
[NTSE]
rows.answer: (d) A primary oocyte begins the first meiotic division, but then arrests until later in life when it will finish this division in a developing follicle. This results in a secondary oocyte, which will complete meiosis if it is fertilized. So, the second meiotic division occurs after fertilisation.

development as well as secretion of estrogens by the growing follicles. Both LH and FSH attain a peak level in the middle of cycle (about 14th day). Rapid secretion of LH leading to its maximum level during the mid-cycle called LH surge induces rupture of Graafian follicle and thereby the release of ovum (ovulation).

rows.title: Check Your Knowledge
rows.question:
Check Your Knowledge
1. Why is regeneration not always same as reproduction?
2. Why can't all multi-cellular organisms reproduce by fragmentation?
3. What is the benefit of reproduction via spore formation?
rows.answer:
1. At times, regeneration is also required to restore a lost part of an individual's body like autotomy in lizards, wound healing, etc. In such cases, organism doesn't actually reproduce. Hence regeneration is not same as reproduction.
2. Multi-cellular organisms have a very complex form of organization (like tissues and organs) which can't be regained by the process of fragmentation.
3. Spores are covered by a hard protective coat of sporopollenin and can survive under unfavourable conditions. Moreover they are small sized; light in weight and can be dispersed easily.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Why is it considered better to grow a rose from cutting rather than from seeds?
rows.answer:
To grow plants by cutting rather than from seeds is a method of artificial asexual reproduction. It is considered better to grow a rose from cutting rather than from seeds due to following reasons:
(i) It is the only method of reproduction in seedless plants.
(ii) We get a fully matured plant in a very short time.
(iii) It is a short cut method for rapid multiplication.
(iv) It helps to preserve the type of character that a plant breeder desires.
(v) It is a very easy and economical method for the multiplication of ornamental plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q2
rows.question: Why does bread mould grow profusely on a moist slice of bread rather than on a dry slice of bread?
rows.answer: Hyphae for digestion of bread nutrients secrete enzymes. For their working and absorption of digested food, hyphae require moisture. Moistened bread slice offers both moisture and nutrients to the bread mould, hence, it grows profusely. Dry slice of bread offers nutrients but not moisture hence, hyphae fail to grow.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Why fertilisation cannot take place in flowers if pollination does not occur?
rows.answer:
In a flower, fertilisation requires both male and female gametes.
If pollination does not occur, male gamete is not available, hence, fertilisation would take place.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Why do mouse parents produce mouse-like offspring ? Give reasons.
rows.answer: Mouse parents produce mouse-like offspring because their reproductive cells contain chromosomes with correctly coded nucleic acid, i.e., reproduction is specific.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Why does an exposed moist piece of bread get covered with a cottony growth?
rows.answer: The moist piece of bread is an ideal substratum for growth of Rhizopus or bread mould. The spores of this fungus are nearly always present in the air. Some of them settle over the bread, germinate and form mycelia which appear as cottony growths. Later on a number of black spots develop. They represent sporangia.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q6
rows.question: Why do green grass plants spring up in dry fields on their own after the rains?
rows.answer: The fields have dry stems of the old grass plants all over. These dry stems have buds in an inactive state. By getting rainwater, the bud present on the dry grass stem gets activated and grow to produce new grass plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q7
rows.question: Why are budding, fragmentation and regeneration all considered as asexual types of reproduction. With neat diagrams explain the process of regeneration in Planaria.
rows.answer:
Budding, fragmentation and regeneration are considered as asexual types of reproduction because:
(i) They involve only one parent,
(ii) Gametes are not involved in reproduction.
(iii) Meiotic division does not take place at any stage of this type of reproduction.
Fig. Regeneration in Planaria

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q8
rows.question: Why is it an advantage for the testes to be situated in the scrotal sac outside the main body cavity? Can you think of one disadvantage?
rows.answer: The testes to be situated in the scrotal sac outside the main body cavity because the formation of sperms require a low temperature than the normal body temperature. Its disadvantage of being outside the body cavity is that it is more prone to injury.

rows.answer: A piece of potato tuber having an eye possesses bud which sprouts to form a new plant. Piece of tuber without an eye does not form a new plant due to absence of bud.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q3
rows.question: A man went to a doctor and the doctor removed his Cowper's gland. How would the removal of cowper's gland affect the man?
rows.answer: Cowper's gland secrete an alkaline fluid which helps to neutralise the acidic environment of the male urethra and female reproductive tract. Removal of cowper's gland thus would inactivate and kill sperms in male urethra and female reproductive tract.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q4

rows.answer:
A few points for prevention of AIDS are:
(i) Use condoms while having sex.
(ii) Pregnant ladies should be checked.
(iii) Use disposable syringe.
(iv) Blood transfusion should be done carefully.
# Case Study Based MCQs (CBSE Sample Issued 2021) Male Reproduction System

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Why is variation beneficial to the species but not necessarily for the individual ?
rows.answer: Variations are differences among individuals produced as a result of errors caused during DNA copying at the time of cell division. If a reproducing organism is suitable to a particular niche and niche drastically changed then the population could be wiped out and got adversely affected. The variants may have the ability to survive under adverse condition. The surviving organism may reproduce and develop a kind of population which is suited for their niche. This shows that the species is able to survive but the individual lacking suitable features failed to do so. Thus, variation is beneficial to the species but not necessarily for the individual.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q2
rows.question: How will an organism be benefitted if it reproduces through spores?
rows.answer:
Spore formation is a method of asexual reproduction which is found in non - flowering plants like fungi (Rhizopus) and bacteria, in which the parent plant produces hundreds of tiny spores which turn into a new plant.
An organism can be benefitted in following ways if it produces through spores:
(i) Spores are thick walled which protect the organism from unfavourable condition and remain dormant till favourable conditions become available.
(ii) Since it is a form of asexual reproduction, so the new organism won't lose its parental characters.
(iii) At a time, several numbers of spores are produced so cannot extinct very easily. Hence, numerous plants can be grown and does not require any medium for dispersal.
(iv) Spores are light weighted so they can be easily transfered from one plant to other for germination.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Can you think of reasons why more complex organisms cannot give rise to new individuals through regeneration ?
rows.answer:
Regeneration is the process carried out by specialised cells which involves the formation of new individual from its body parts. These cells proliferate and make large numbers of cells. From this mass of cells, different
cells undergo changes to become various cell types and tissues. Since the simple organisms comprise of only one type of cells which assist that organism to grow and develop in Hydra and Planaria.
Complex organism have organ - system level of organisation in which all the organs of organ system work as an interconnected unit. They can regenerate their body parts such as skin, muscles etc. but cannot give rise to new individual through regeneration.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Why is vegetative propagation practised for growing some types of plants?
rows.answer:
Vegetative propagation is practiced for growing certain types of plants because:
(i) It is the only method of reproduction in those plants which have lost their capacity to produce seeds like banana, orange, rose, jasmine etc.
(ii) Plants raised by vegetative propagation can bear flowers and fruits earlier than those produced from seeds.
(iii) Plants produced by this method are genetically similar enough to the parent plant to have all its characteristics.
(iv) Vegetative propagation is used in methods such as layering or grafting to grow many plants like sugarcane, roses, or grapes for agricultural purposes.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Why is DNA copying an essential part of the process of reproduction?
rows.answer: DNA copying is the biological process of producing two identical copies of DNA from one original DNA molecule. DNA are the carriers of genetic information which transfers the information or characters from parents to offspring. It also helps to generates variations during sexual reproduction and leads to evolution. This explains how DNA copying is an essential part of the process of replication.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q6
rows.question: How is the process of pollination different from fertilization?
rows.answer:
Difference between pollination and fertilisation are as follows-
| S.No. | Pollination | Fertilisation |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (i) | Pollination is the process of transferring pollen grains from the male anther to female stigma. | Fertilisation is fusion of male and female gametes to form a new organism of the same species. |
| (ii) | It occurs in the early stages of sexual reproduction in all flowering plants. | It is preceded by the pollination in all flowering plants. |
| (iii) | It requires external factors like wind, birds, insects, animals etc. | It does not require any external factor and can be achieved by the growth of the pollen tube. |
| (iv) | In pollination, there is no formation of pollen tube occur. | In fertilisation, pollen tube is formed for transferring male gametes into an egg cell. |
| (v) | Pollination occurs only in plants. | Fertilisation occurs in almost in every living organism like plants, humans, animals, insects etc. |

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q7
rows.question: What is the role of the seminal vesicles and the prostate gland?
rows.answer:
Seminal vesicles are one pair which lies at the base of urinary bladder behind its neck. They secrete a viscous fluid for nourishment of sperms in human male.
Prostate Gland : It is single in number which surrounds the first part of urethra. Its secretion is poured into urethra by many ducts.
It secretes alkaline fluid which is discharged into urethra and neutralize any acidity caused due to residual urine. Both the parts add their secreations so that the sperms mix with the fluid which makes their transport easier.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q8
rows.question: What are the changes seen in girls at the time of puberty ?
rows.answer:
The changes seen in girls at the time of puberty are as follows:
- Pelvic region and the overall fat content of the body increases.
- The waist narrows due to more deposition of fat on the hips under the influence of hormones.
- Mammary gland starts developing with dark nipples at the tip of breast.
- Pubic hairs appear in the genital area, underarms, face, hands, and legs.
- At the time of puberty, menstrual cycle begins.
- The size of uterus and ovary increases.
- More secretion of oil from the skin, which results in the appearance of pimples.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q9
rows.question: If a woman is using a copper-T, will it help in protecting her from sexually transmitted diseases?
rows.answer:
- No, copper-T will not help a woman to protect her from sexually transmitted disease.
- The copper-T is a simple intra-uterine device (IUD) made of a flexible, "T" shaped piece of plastic wrapped with a thin copper containing wire. It only prevents the implantation of the embryo in the uterus. As copper- T does not prevent entry of semen so it is not able to protect a woman from sexually transmitted diseases (STDs).

rows.answer: (c) Ovary, uterus and fallopian tube are the part of female reproductive system but vas deferens is a part of male reproductive system in human beings. The sperms are delivered through the vas deferens which unites with a tube coming from the urinary bladder.

rows.answer: (d) Stamens are the male reproductive parts of flowers. A stamen consists of an anther (which produces pollen) and a filament.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q3
rows.question: Why does menstruation occur ?
rows.answer: Menstruation, or period, is normal vaginal bleeding that occurs as part of a woman's monthly cycle. Every month, one egg is released by an ovary and if the egg is not fertilised, it lives for about one day. Since the ovary releases one egg every month, the uterus also prepares itself every month to receive a fertilised egg. Thus its lining becomes thick and spongy. The main purpose of this lining is to provide nourishment to the embryo. Now, however, this lining is not needed any longer. So, the lining slowly breaks and comes out through the vagina as blood and mucous which causes menstruation.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q5
rows.question: How does reproduction help in providing stability to populations of species?
rows.answer:
Reproduction is a life process which enables an organism to reproduce its own offspring. Thus, they continue their species without extinction. Reproduction helps in providing stability to populations of species by considering following points:
- It provides stability to population of species by giving birth to new individuals as the rate of birth must be at par with the rate of death to provide stability to population of a species.
- The consistency of DNA copying during reproduction is also important for the maintenance of body design features that allow the organism to use that particular niche. Therefore, reproduction is linked to the stability of populations of species.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q6
rows.question: What are the functions performed by the testes in human beings?
rows.answer:
Testes is a part of both male reproductive system and endocrine system. They are located behind the penis in a pouch of skin called the scrotum. The functions performed by the testes in human beings are as follows:
- It helps in the production of sperms in male which takes part in sexual reproduction.
- It assists in the secretion of the testosterone hormone, which plays a key role in the development of secondary sexual characters in male.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q7
rows.question: How are the modes of reproduction different in unicellular and multicellular organisms?
rows.answer:
Unicellular organisms are single celled organisms in which all the physiological activities like reproduction, respiration excretion etc. are performed by a single cell. Multicellular organisms are those which consist of more than one cell, in contrast to unicellular organisms. Therefore, modes of reproduction are different in unicellular and multicellular organism as:
- Unicellular organisms always reproduce by asexual methods like budding, regeneration, spore formation, binary fission, multiple fission, etc. Multicellular organism, on the other hand, uses both sexual and asexual method for reproduction.
- In unicellular organism, there is only single cell which perform all the activities so there is no specialised cells and tissues that are meant for reproduction. But in multicellular organism, they have well developed reproductive organ which takes part in reproduction.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q8
rows.question: What could be the reasons for adopting contraceptive methods?
rows.answer:
Contraceptive method is any method, medicine, or device used to prevent pregnancy. It includes mechanical barrier method, hormonal method, surgical method, IUDs, etc.
The reasons for adopting contraceptive methods are as follows:
D
E
(i) Population control - It helps in controlling the population rate or birth rate.
(ii) Prevents unwanted pregnancies - It helps to maintain a women's health by preventing unwanted pregnancies and by keeping gap between consecutive pregnancies.
(iii) Prevents from STDs - There are few contraceptive methods like condoms which prevents spread of sexually transmitted diseases and lethal diseases like HIV/AIDS.
(iv) It assists in family planning - Family planning/ contraception reduces the need for abortion, especially unsafe abortion.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q1
rows.question:
Which of the following is the correct sequence of events of sexual reproduction in a flower?
(a) Pollination, fertilisation, seedling, embryo
(b) Seedling, embryo, fertilisation, pollination
(c) Pollination, fertilisation, embryo, seedling
(d) Embryo, seedling, pollination, fertilisation
rows.answer: (c) In pollination, pollen grains transfer from stamen to stigma after which fertilization takes place during which germ cells fuse together to form zygote which in turn leads to embryo formation. Fertilized ovule becomes seed and seeds germinate to produce seedling.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q16

Stem

rows.answer: (c) Intracytoplasmic Sperm Injection (ICSI) is an in vitro fertilisation procedure in which a single sperm is injected directly into an egg. This procedure is most commonly used to overcome male infertility problems, although it may also be used where eggs cannot easily be penetrated by sperm, and occasionally in addition to sperm donation.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q17
rows.question:
If a starfish is cut into pieces, each piece grow into a complete animal. The process is called
(a) regeneration
(b) reproduction
(c) healing of wounds
(d) growth
rows.answer: (c) Synergids have filiform apparatus. A synergid cell wall forms a highly thickened structure called the filiform apparatus at the micropylar end consisting of numerous finger-like projections into synergid cytoplasm. These synergid cells are necessary for pollen tube guidance in ovule.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q18

(a) Nucleus of bud, bud, Yeast, nucleus
(b) Dividing nucleus of bud, bud, Yeast, nucleus
(c) Nucleus of bud, bud, Yeast, dividing nucleus of yeast
(d) Dividing nucleus of Yeast, Yeast, bud, nucleus of bud.

(a) Seminal fluid
(b) Vaginal fluid
(c) Uterine lining
(d) Fallopian tube
rows.answer: (d) The hilum is a scar found on the seed coat through which the developing seeds are attached to the fruit.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q20
rows.question:
If both ovaries are removed from a rat, then which hormone is decreased in blood
(a) Oxytocin
(b) Oestrogen
(c) Prolactin
(d) Gonadotrophin
rows.answer: (d) The hilum is a scar found on the seed coat through which the developing seeds are attached to the fruit.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q21
rows.question:
Which part of the ovary in mammals acts as an endocrine gland after ovulation?
(a) Vitelline membrane
(b) Graafian follicles
(c) Stroma
(d) Germinal epithelium
rows.answer: (a) Pollen grains of different species are incompatible, so they fail to germinate. If the pollen-stigma interaction is compatible, the pollen grain hydrates and germinates shortly following landing on the stigma.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q22
rows.question:
In budding,
(a) Outgrowth develops earlier than nuclear division
(b) Nucleus divides earlier than development of outgrowth
(c) Both occur simultaneously
(d) There is no fixed sequence
rows.answer: (a) Tapetum is important for the nutrition and development of pollen grains, as well as a source of precursors for the pollen coat.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q23
rows.question:
A student upon observing a permanent slide of binary fission in Amoeba drew the following diagram. Identify the fault in the diagram:
(a) Size of daughter cell to be formed is not appropriate.
(b) Constriction has appeared before nuclear division.
(c) Constriction is not equally deep on the either side.
(d) None of the above
rows.answer: (b) The word 'ampulla' is derived from the Latin word 'flask'. Being the second portion of the fallopian tube, it is the intermediate dilated portion which immediately curves over the ovary. This is the common site of human fertilisation as both the ovum and sperms are simultaneously transported here.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q24

In the process of binary fission:
(a) Cytoplasm divides first followed by division of nucleus
(b) Nucleus divides first followed by division of cytoplasm
(c) Both nucleus and cytoplasm divide simultaneously
(d) Cell protrudes followed by division of nucleus
rows.answer: (c) Capacitation increases fertilising capacity of sperms and occurs in female reproductive tract. It is required to render sperm to fertilise an oocyte.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q25

(C)
(D)

(b) A, C, A, B
(c) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mi>B</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>D</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>A</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>C</mi></mrow></math>
(d) C, A, D, B
rows.answer: (b) In a majority of flowering plants, one of the megaspore is functional while the other three degenerate. Only functional megaspore develops into female gametophyte (embryo sac), this is known as monosporic development.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q26
rows.question:
(A): Vigrous contraction of the uterus at the end of pregnancy causes expulsion.
(B): The stimulatory reflex between the uterine contraction and oxytocin results in weakening contractions.
(a) Both the statements (A) and (B) are correct
(b) Statement (A) is correct but (B) is incorrect.
(c) Both the statements (A) and (B) are incorrect
(d) Statement(B) is correct but (A) is incorrect.
rows.answer: (c) Double fertilisation is a unique phenomenon that occurs in angiosperms only.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q27
rows.question:
(A): The middle piece is called as power house of the sperm.
(B): The numerous mitochondria coiled around axial filament produce energy for the movement of the tail.
(a) Both the statements (A) and (B) are correct
(b) Statement (A) is correct but (B) is incorrect.
(c) Both the statements (A) and (B) are incorrect
(d) Statement(B) is correct but (A) is incorrect.
rows.answer: (d) The phenomenon in which female gamete develops into embryo without getting fused with male gamete (fertilisation) is called parthenogenesis. Autogamy is a form of self-fertilisation in which fission of the cell nucleus occurs without division of the cell, the two pronuclei so formed reuniting to form the synkaryon. Parthenocarpy is the production of fruit without fertilisation of an egg in the ovary. Synkaryon is a nucleus formed by the fusion of two pre-existing nuclei.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q28
rows.question:
Which of the following cells during gametogenesis is normally dipoid?
(a) Spermatid
(b) Spermatogonia
(c) Secondary polar body
(d) Primary polar body
rows.answer:
(d) Post-fertilisation is a series of events that takes place after fertilisation. Following are the postfertilisation changes in flowering plants:
- Ovule develops into Seed
- Ovary develops into Fruit
- Zygote develops into Embryo
- Central cell develops into Endosperm

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q29
rows.question:
In human females, meiosis II is not complete until?
(a) Fertilisation
(b) Uterine implantation
(c) Birth
(d) Puberty
rows.answer: (c) In double fertilization, one male gamete fuses with the egg and results in the formation of a diploid zygote and this process is called fertilization. The other male gamete fuses with the two polar nuclei to form a triploid endosperm. This process is called triple fusion. After fertilisation, the fertilized ovule forms the seed while the tissues of the ovary become the fruit.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q30
rows.question:
The human embryo gets nutrition from the mother blood with the help of a special organ called
[NTSE]
(a) Zygote
(b) Ovary
(c) Oviduct
(d) Placenta
[NTSE]
rows.answer: (d) A primary oocyte begins the first meiotic division, but then arrests until later in life when it will finish this division in a developing follicle. This results in a secondary oocyte, which will complete meiosis if it is fertilized. So, the second meiotic division occurs after fertilisation.

rows.answer:
(d) The sperm releases from the testis, enters into epididymis which leads to vas deferens. Then sperms are transferred into the urethra.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q34
rows.question:
"Double fertilization" is a complex mechanism of flowering plants that is also unique to angiosperms. Choose the most appropriate statement from the options listed below that explains this phenomenon.
[NTSE]
(a) Fertilization in two flowers of the same plant forming endosperms.
(b) Two male gametes fertilize two eggs inside the ovule as a result the ovary gives rise to bigger fruits.
(c) Two fertilizations occur in a flower-one fertilization results in the formation of a diploid zygote and the second fertilization results in the formation of a triploid endosperm.
(d) Two pollen grains sending two pollen tubes inside the ovary, resulting in the formation of two seeds inside the fruit.
rows.answer:

[JSTSE]

(b) Zygote develops into embryo.
(c) Central cell develops into endosperm.
(d) Ovules develop into embryo sac.

[KVPY]
(a) before fertilisation
(b) after implantation
(c) before ovulation
(d) after fertilisation
rows.answer:
(b) The correct sequence for transport of sperm cells in male reproductive system is:
Seminiferoustubules → Rete testis → Vasa efferentia
→ Epididymis → Vas deferens → Ejaculatory duct
→ Urethra → Urethral meatus

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs): — Q36
rows.question:
The correct route that sperm follows when it releases from the testis of a mammal:
[NTSE]
(a) Vas deferens → Epididymis → Urethra
(b) Urethra → Epididymis → Vas deferens
(c) Epididymis → Urethra → Vas deferens
(d) Epididymis → Vas deferens → Urethra
rows.answer:
(d) The gonadotropin (LH and FSH) increases gradually during the follicular phase, and stimulates follicular
development as well as secretion of estrogens by the growing follicles. Both LH and FSH attain a peak level in the middle of cycle (about 14th day). Rapid secretion of LH leading to its maximum level during the mid-cycle called LH surge induces rupture of Graafian follicle and thereby the release of ovum (ovulation).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q1
rows.question:
A basket of vegetables contains carrot, potato, radish and tomato. Which of them represent the correct homologous structures?
(a) Carrot and potato
(b) Carrot and tomato
(c) Radish and carrot
(d) Radish and potato
rows.answer: (c) Homologous organs perform different functions and have different appearance but share common basic structural plan and origin.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q2
rows.question:
According to the evolutionary theory, formation of a new species is generally due to:
(a) Sudden creation by nature
(b) Accumulation of variations over several generations
(c) Clones formed during asexual reproduction
(d) Movement of individuals from one habitat to another
rows.answer: (b) Accumulation of variations over several generations serve as raw material on which evolutionary force (natural selection) acts upon to create new species.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q3
rows.question:
From the list given below, select the character which can be acquired but not inherited:
(a) Colour of eye
(b) Colour of skin
(c) Size of body
(d) Nature of hair
rows.answer: (c) Environmentally determined traits are acquired. Size of body depends on nutritional status, health conditions etc.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q4
rows.question:
Select the group which shares maximum number of common characters.
(a) Two individuals of a species
(b) Two species of a genus
(c) Two genera of a family
(d) Two genera of two families
rows.answer: (a) Species form the lowest taxonomic category and includes all the organisms that share maximum number of common characters. While moving up in taxonomic hierarchy, number of shared characters get reduced.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Why do all the gametes formed in human females have an X chromosome?
rows.answer: Human females have two X chromosomes called sex chromosomes. During meiosis, at the time of gamete formation, one X chromosome enters each gamete. Hence all the gametes possess an X chromosome.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q6
rows.question: Do genetic combination of mothers play a significant role in determining the sex of a new born?
rows.answer: No, because mothers have a pair of X-chromosomes. All children will inherit an ' X ' chromosome from their mother regardless of whether they are boys or girls.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q7
rows.question: In human beings, the statistical probability of getting either a male or female child is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>50</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>50</mn></mrow></math>. Give a suitable explanation.
rows.answer: The sex of an infant is determined by the type of sex chromosome contributed by the male gamete. Since the ratio of male gametes containing X chromosome and those containing Y chromosome is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>50</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>50</mn></mrow></math>, the statistical probability of male or a female infant is also <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>50</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>50</mn></mrow></math>.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q8
rows.question: A very small population of a species faces a greater threat of extinction than a larger population. Provide a suitable genetic explanation.
rows.answer:
Smaller populations are at greater threat of extinction as compared to large species. This is because less number of individual in smaller population imparts lesser changes in production of genetic variations. Lack of genetic diversity makes the smaller species more prone to extinction under any dramatic changes in prevailing environmental conditions.
On the other hand, large populations exhibit more genetic diversity on which evolutionary forces act upon to make it withstand any changes in surrounding.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q9
rows.question: A woman has only daughters. Analyse the situation genetically and provide a suitable explanation.
rows.answer:
The male individual contains one X chromosome and one Y chromosome (XY) and the female individual contains two X chromosomes (XX). Genetically we can prove female have two X chromosomes.
- If a sperm carrying <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mi>X</mi></mrow></math> chromosome fertilizes an egg then the offspring will be a female child. This is because the offspring will have XX combination of sex chromosomes. If a sperm carrying Y chromosome fertilizes an egg then the offspring will be a male child. This is because the offspring will have XY combination of sex chromosomes. You will find that the sex of the child is determined by the type of sperm that fuses with the ovum at the time of fertilization.
- Male produces two different kinds of gametes. Half of the gametes having <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mi>X</mi></mrow></math> chromosomes and other half of the gametes having Y chromosomes. Therefore males are heterogametic. Female therefore produces same type of all gametes. So, females are homogametic.
If a woman has only daughters, we can understand that every time the male X chromosome only fused with the female X chromosome. And all the offspring with XX combination of sex chromosomes became her daughters.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: Passage Based Questions: \section*{Passage} — Exemplar Questions: — Q10
rows.question: All the human races like Africans, Asians, Europeans, Americans and others might have evolved from a common ancestor. Provide a few evidences in support of this view.
rows.answer:
Human races are anthropogenic concept; it has no roots in biology. Body plan, morphology, anatomy, physiology and metabolism is common to all human. They are share constant chromosome number (23 pairs) and same genetic as almost <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>99.9</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> DNA is same in all human races. And also all the human race can freely inter-breed to produce fertile offspring.
Evolutionary events also indicate origin of human from a common African ancestor. The earliest members of the human species, Homo sapiens can be traced there. Taking into account of all these evidences, all human races seem to have common ancestor.

Wind of bat and wing of bird are the example of the
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi></mrow></math> organs.
rows.answer: (b) Allopatric speciation, also referred to as geographic speciation or its earlier name, the dumbbell model, is a mode of speciation that occurs when biological populations of the same species become isolated from each other to an extent that prevents or interferes with gene flow.

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — Fill in the Blanks: — Q23
rows.question:
Forelimbs of frog and lizard are the example of the
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi><mi>_</mi></mrow></math> organs.
rows.answer: (b) The correct option is (b) because herbicide resistant weeds, drug resistant eukaryotes and man-created breeds of domesticated animals like dogs are examples of evolution by anthropogenic action. Darwin's Finches of Galapagos islands are example of natural selection, adaptive radiation and founder's effect.

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q1
rows.question: Mouth parts of insects show divergent evolution.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q2
rows.question: Life can originate on earth from pre-existing life only.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q3
rows.question: The atmosphere of the primitive earth was reducing.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q4
rows.question: Variations arising during the process of reproduction cannot be inherited.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q5
rows.question: Sex is determined by different factors in various species.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q6
rows.question: Changes in the non-reproductive tissues caused by environmental factors are inheritable.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q7
rows.question: Exchange of genetic material takes place in asexual reproduction.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q8
rows.question: A cross between a true tall and pure dwarf pea plant resulted in production of all tall plants because tallness is the dominant trait.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q9
rows.question: Reduction in weight of an organism due to nutrition is genetically controlled.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q10
rows.question: New species may be formed if DNA undergoes significant changes or chromosome number changes in the gametes.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q11
rows.question: Both the parents contribute DNA equally to the offspring.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q12
rows.question: Sex of the child is determined by the type of ovum provided by the mother.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q13
rows.question: A recessive trait can also be common as blood group O .
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q14
rows.question: Attached ear lobe is recessive trait.
rows.answer: True

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — True / False: — Q15
rows.question: Charles Darwin discovered the law of independent assortment.
rows.answer: False

rows.title: Assertion \& Reason: — Match the Following: — Q1
rows.question:
Match the genetic cross of the parents on the left with the genotypes of the offspring most likely to be produced from that cross on the right.
