rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q35
rows.question:
Match the following columns and select the correct
| | Column-I | | Column-II |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| A. | Non-parental gene exchange | I. | Crossing over |
| B. | Non-sister chromatids | II. | X and Y |
| C. | Sex chromosome | III. | Autosome-linked disease |
| D. | Thalassaemia | IV. | Recombination |
rows.answer: (a) Crossing over takes place only between non-sister chromatids.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q36
rows.question: In order to find out different types of gametes produced by a pea plant having the genotype AaBb , it should be
rows.answer: (c) In order to find out the gamete or the genotype of an unknown individual, scientists perform a test cross. In test cross, the individual in question is crossed with the homozygous recessive parent.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q37
rows.question:
(A): A good example of multiple alleles is ABO blood group system.
(B): When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> alleles are present together in ABO blood group system, they both express their own types.
(a) Statement (A) and (B) both are correct.
(b) Statement (A) is correct but (B) is incorrect.
(c) Statement (A) is incorrect but (B) is correct
(d) Statement (A) and (B) both are incorrect.
rows.answer: (a) ABO system consists of four blood groups-A, B, AB and O . ABO blood groups are controlled by gene I. The gene has three alleles <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><mo>,</mo><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and i . This phenomenon is known as multiple allelism. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> are completely dominant over i . When <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> are present together, they both express themselves and produce AB blood group. This phenomenon is known as co-dominance.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q38
rows.question:
Suppose that in sheep, a dominant allele (B) produces black hair and a recessive allele (b) produces white hair. If you saw a black sheep, you would be able to identify
(a) its phenotype for hair colour.
(b) its genotype for hair colour.
(c) the genotypes for only one of its parents.
(d) the genotypes for both of its parents.
rows.answer: (a) Black is the phenotype of the sheep. Without further information, you cannot identify the genotype of a black sheep because it could be either BB or Bb . The possible genotypes of the parents of a black sheep could <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mo>×</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mo>,</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi></mrow><mo>×</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mo>,</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mo>×</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mrow></math>, or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow><mo>×</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mrow></math>.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q39
rows.question:
Refer the given statements and select the correct option.
(i) Percentage of homozygous dominant individuals obtained by selfing Aa individuals is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>25</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>.
(ii) Types of genetically different gametes produced by genotype AABbcc are 2.
(iii) Phenotypic ratio of monohybrid <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>F</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> progeny in case of Mirabilis jalapa is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>.
(a) All the statements are correct.
(b) Statements (i) and (ii) are true, but statement (iii) is false.
(c) Statements (i) and (iii) are true, but statement (ii) is false.
(d) Statements (ii) and (iii) are true, but statement (i) is false.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q40
rows.question:
In a case of mammalian coat color, the principal gene identified is ' C ' which codes for a tyrosinase enzyme. In case of rabbits four different phenotypes are observed Full Color > Chinchilla > Himalayan > Albino (in order of the expression of gene ' C' and its alleles). In a progeny obtained after crossing two rabbits, the percentages of Chinchilla, Himalayan and Albino rabbits were 50, 25 and 25 respectively. What must have been the genotypes of the parent rabbits?
(a) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><mo>×</mo><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>
(b) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><mo>×</mo><msup><mi>X</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>
(c) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><msup><mi>c</mi><mrow><mi>a</mi></mrow></msup><mo>×</mo><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>
(d) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><mo>×</mo><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup></mrow></math>
rows.answer:
(c) Here :
Genotype of Full Colour - <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mtext>ch </mtext></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mtext>ch </mtext></mrow></msup></mrow></math>
Genotype of Chinchilla - <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></mrow></msup><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>
Genotype of Himalayan - <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> or <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup><mi>c</mi></mrow></math>
Genotype of Albino - cc

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q41
rows.question:
The gene for hemophilia is present on X chromosome. If a hemophilic male marries a normal female, the probability of their son being hemophilic is
[NTSE]
(a) nil
(b) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>25</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>
(c) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>50</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>
(d) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>100</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>
rows.answer:
(a)
Since all the male progeny will get the X chromosome form their mother, they will all be normal.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q42
rows.question:
In the experiment conducted by Mendel, RRyy (round green) and rrYY (wrinkled, yellow) seeds of pea plant were used. In the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>F</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> generation 240 progeny were produced, out of which 15 progeny had specific characteristics. What were the characteristics?
[NTSE]
(a) round and green
(b) round and yellow
(c) wrinkle and yellow
(d) wrinkle and green

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q43
rows.question:
A breeder crossed a pure bred tall plant having white flowers to a pure bred short plant having blue flowers. He obtained <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>202</mn><msub><mrow><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">F</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> progeny and found that they are all tall having white flowers. Upon selfing these <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>F</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> plants, he obtained a progeny of 2160 plants. Approximately, how many of these are likely to be short and having blue flowers?
[KVPY]
(a) 1215
(b) 405
(c) 540
(d) 135
rows.answer:
(d) According to dihybrid phenotypic ratio
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>9</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>,
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mtable><mtr><mtd columnalign="left"><mtext> TW - </mtext><mn>9</mn></mtd><mtd columnalign="left"><mtext> Tw - </mtext><mn>3</mn></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd columnalign="left"><mtext> tW - </mtext><mn>3</mn></mtd><mtd columnalign="left"><mtext> tw - </mtext><mn>1</mn></mtd></mtr></mtable></mrow></math>
The total number of short and blue flowered plants is
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>16</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>×</mo><mn>2130</mn><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1080</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>8</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>=</mo><mn>135</mn></mrow></math>

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q44
rows.question:
Varieties of vegetables such as cabbage, broccoli and cauliflower have been produced from a wild cabbage species. Such process of producing new varieties of living organisms is called
[NTSE]
(a) Natural selection
(b) Artificial selection
(c) Speciation
(d) Genetic drift
rows.answer: (b) Artificial selection is characterized by intentional reproduction of an organism having desirable traits. It is also called as selective breeding.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q45
rows.question:
Which of the following are pairs of analogous organs?
(I) Forelimbs of horse - Wings of bat
[NTSE]
(II) Wings of bat -Wings of butterfly
(III) Forelimbs of horse - Wings of butterfly
(IV) Wings of bird - Wings of bat
(a) (I) and (II)
(b) (II) and (IV)
(c) (III) and (IV)
(d) (II) and (III)
rows.answer: (b) Analogous organs are such organs which show anatomically different structures but doing similar functions. Example : wings of a bat and wings of a pigeon.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q46
rows.question:
Of the periods listed below, which one is the earliest period when ostracoderms, the jawless and finless fishes, appeared?
[KVPY]
(a) Devonian period
(b) Cambrian period
(c) Carboniferous period
(d) Silurian period

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q47
rows.question:
Which one of the following options lists the primary energy source(s) for all forms of life on the earth?
(a) Light, inorganic substances
[KVPY]
(b) Inorganic substances, organic substances
(c) Light, organic substances
(d) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>
rows.answer: (b) Chromosomes carry genes, which are the hereditary characters to the offspring.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q48
rows.question:
Four important events given below may have led to the origin of life on the earth.
[NTSE]
(A) Formation of amino acids and nucleotides
(B) Availability of water
(C) Organization of Cells
(D) Formation of complex molecules
(a) A, B, C and D
(b) B, A, D and C
(b) A, D, B and C
(d) B, C, A and D
rows.answer:
(a) The genotypes of offspring of parents having <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> blood groups are:
From the above cross, it is shown that none of the offspring will be of blood group O.
∴ The probability of their first child having type O blood is zero.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q49
rows.question:
Which of the following carry hereditary characters to the off spring in the organism?
[NTSE]
(a) Ribosome
(b) Chromosome
(c) Plasma
(d) Lysosome
rows.answer:
Which of the following carry hereditary characters to the off spring in the organism?
[NTSE]
(a) Ribosome
(b) Chromosome
(c) Plasma
(d) Lysosome

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q50
rows.question:
If the genotypes determining the blood groups of a couple are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math>, then the probability of their first child having type O blood is:
[KVPY]
(a) 0
(b) 0.25
(c) 0.50
(d) 0.75
rows.answer:
If the genotypes determining the blood groups of a couple are <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow></msup><msup><mi>I</mi><mrow><mi>B</mi></mrow></msup></mrow></math>, then the probability of their first child having type O blood is:
[KVPY]
(a) 0
(b) 0.25
(c) 0.50
(d) 0.75

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q51
rows.question:
A cross was carried out between two individuals heterozygous for two pairs of genes. Assuming segregation and independent assortment, the number of different genotypes and phenotypes obtained respectively would be:
[KVPY]
(a) 4 and 9
(b) 6 and 3
(c) 9 and 4
(d) 11 and 4
rows.answer:
(c) In the given question, both parents are heterozygous for two pairs of genes. This means the cross is a dihybrid cross.
Let us assume a dihybrid cross,
| | YR | Yr | yR | yr |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| YR | YYRR yellow round | YYRr yellow round | YyRR yellow round | YyRr yellow round |
| Yr | YYRr yellow round | Yyrr yellow wrinkled | YyRr yellow round | Yyrr yellow wrinkled |
| yR | YyRR yellow round | YyRr yellow round | yyRR green round | yyRr green round |
| yr | YyRr yellow round | Yyrr yellow wrinkled | yyRr green round | yyrr green wrinkled |
The genotype ratio is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>4</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math> The phenotypic ratio is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>9</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>
∴ The number of different genotypes and phenotypes obtained would be 9 and 4 , respectively.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q52
rows.question:
A plant with red coloured flowers is crossed with a plant having white flowers. The red and white colour of the flower is controlled by a single gene. Red is dominant over white. The <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mi>F</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></math> progeny is self-pollinated and the flower colour in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>F</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> is observed. Given the above information, what is the expected phenotypic ratio of plants with different flower colours?
[NTSE]
(a) All plants with red flowers
(b) Red : white in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>
(c) Pink : white in the ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>
(d) Red : pink : white in a ratio of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>2</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>
rows.answer:
(b) Phenotypic ratio can be determined by doing a test cross and identifying the frequency of a trait or trait combinations that will be expressed based on the genotypes of the offspring.
F2 generation
| • | R | I |
| :---: | :---: | :---: |
| (R) | RR Red | Rr Red |
| (r) | RR Red | rr white |
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mtable><mtr><mtd columnalign="center"><mtext> Phenotype : </mtext></mtd><mtd columnalign="center"><mtext> Red : White </mtext></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd columnalign="center"><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mtd></mtr></mtable></mrow></math>

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q53
rows.question:
Which one of the following is a correct statement about primates evolution?
[KVPY]
(a) Chimpanzees and gorillas evolved from macaques
(b) Humans and chimpanzees evolved from gorillas
(c) Humans, chimpanzees and gorillas evolved from a common ancestor
(d) Humans and gorillas evolved from chimpanzees
rows.answer: (c) According to the new genetic research-when combined with known fossils-the lineage that led to humans, chimps, and gorillas evolved from a common ancestor about 10 million years ago.

rows.title: Exercise 3 — Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs) : — Q54
rows.question:
The gene for the genetic disease "Haemophilia" is present on the ' X ' chromosome. If a haemophilic male marries a normal female, what would be the probability of their son being haemophilic.
[NTSE]
(a) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>50</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>
(b) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>100</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>
(c) Nil
(d) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>:</mi><mn>1</mn></mrow></math>
rows.answer:
(c)
Foundation Builder +

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q1
rows.question:
Organisms which synthesise carbohydrates from inorganic compounds using radiant energy are called
(a) decomposers
(b) producers
(c) herbivores
(d) carnivores
rows.answer:
(b) Organisms which synthesise carbohydrates from inorganic compounds using radiant energy are called producers e.g., all green plants. The producers make the energy from sunlight available to the rest of the ecosystem.
Organisms which consume the food produced, either directly from producers or indirectly by feeding on other consumers are the consumers. Microorganisms which break-down the complex organic substances into simple inorganic substances used by plants are called decomposers. Carnivores and herbivores are types of consumers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q2
rows.question:
Which of the statement is incorrect?
(a) All green plants and blue-green algae are producers.
(b) Green plants get their food from organic compounds.
(c) Producers prepare their own food from inorganic compounds.
(d) Plants convert solar energy into chemical energy.
rows.answer: (b) Producers capture the solar energy and convert it into chemical energy. All green plants and certain blue-green algae which can produce food by photosynthesis come under this category and are called the producer.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q3
rows.question:
Which of the following limits the number of trophic levels in a food chain?
(a) Decrease in energy at higher trophic levels.
(b) Deficient food supply.
(c) Polluted air.
(d) Water.
rows.answer: (a) There is a loss of energy as we go from one trophic level to the next, this limits the number of trophic levels in a food-chain.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q4
rows.question:
In the given diagram, the various trophic levels are shown in a pyramid. At which trophic level is maximum energy available?
(a) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>T</mi><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>
(b) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>T</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>
(c) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>T</mi><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>
(d) <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>T</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q5
rows.question:
What will happen if deer is missing in the food chain given below?
Grass → Deer → Tiger
(a) The population of tiger increases.
(b) The population of grass decreases.
(c) Tiger will start eating grass.
(d) The population of tiger decreases and the population of grass increases.
rows.answer:
Following are the four activities which are eco-friendly?
(i) Separation of biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.
(ii) Gardening
(iii) Use of gunny bags/paper bags in place of polythene/plastic bags.
(iv) Use of compost and vermicompost in place of fertilisers
(v) Harvesting rain water.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q6
rows.question: Suggest any four activities in daily life which are ecofriendly.
rows.answer:
Wastes
(i) Vegetable and fruit peels and skin, stale food, food leftovers, used tea leaves.
(ii) Milk pouches, polythene bags, empty cartons.
(iii) Waste paper (newspaper, bags, envelopes), packing paper, empty bottles, torn cloth pieces, etc.
(iv) Dust and other sweepings.
Disposal :
(i) Separation into biodegradable and nonbiodegradable, recyclable and non-recyclable wastes.
(ii) Recyclable wastes (waste paper, cloth, polythene or plastic bags, cartouns, bottles, cans, etc.) can be given to rag pickers for recycling.
(iii) Preparation of compost or vermicompost from kitchen wastes for home garden (kitchen garden).
(iv) In the absence of kitchen garden, the household garbage and other wastes can be given to waste collectors for disposal.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q7
rows.question: Name the wastes which are generated in your house daily. What measures would you take for their disposal?
rows.answer: (d) Global warming is increase of earth's average temperature due to greenhouse effect which is naturally occurring phenomenon that is responsible for heating of Earth's surface and atmosphere. The main greenhouse gas is carbon-dioxide (contribute <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>60</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> ). In first three options, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> concentration increases but it decreases in option (d).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q8
rows.question: Give two differences between food chain and food web.
rows.answer:
| S.No. | Food cain | Food web |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| (i) | Food chain is a series of organisms feeding on one another. | Food web consists of a number of interlinked food chains. |
| (ii) | Members of higher trophic level feed upon a single type of organism of the lower trophic level. | Members of higher trophic level can feed upon organisms of the lower trophic levels of other food chain. |

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q9
rows.question: What are the advantages of cloth bags over plastic bags during shopping?
rows.answer:
Cloth bags are
(i) capable of carrying more things
(ii) made of biodegradable material
(iii) do not pollute our environment
(iv) can be reused.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q10
rows.question: Why are crop fields known as artificial ecosystems?
rows.answer:
Artificial ecosystems are those ecosystems which are modified and managed by human beings for their own benefit.
Crop fields are not like wild forest area, the piece of land which has to converted into crop field is manged. The soil is prepared for sowing seeds, then irrigated and further also kept under observation for getting good yield of crops. Thus, crop field is an artificial ecosystem.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Exemplar Questions: — Q6
rows.question:
Wastes
(i) Vegetable and fruit peels and skin, stale food, food leftovers, used tea leaves.
(ii) Milk pouches, polythene bags, empty cartons.
(iii) Waste paper (newspaper, bags, envelopes), packing paper, empty bottles, torn cloth pieces, etc.
(iv) Dust and other sweepings.
Disposal :
(i) Separation into biodegradable and nonbiodegradable, recyclable and non-recyclable wastes.
(ii) Recyclable wastes (waste paper, cloth, polythene or plastic bags, cartouns, bottles, cans, etc.) can be given to rag pickers for recycling.
(iii) Preparation of compost or vermicompost from kitchen wastes for home garden (kitchen garden).
(iv) In the absence of kitchen garden, the household garbage and other wastes can be given to waste collectors for disposal.

(d) Ecological pyramid is a graphical representation that illustrates the flow of energy through different forms of life in an ecosystem. It is generally inverted in case of pyramid of biomass in a sea because the biomass of the fishes is more than the phytoplanktons (producers).
Features of pyramid of biomass in a sea:
- Food chain in ocean - Phytoplankton → zooplankton → predatory fish
- The biomass of consumers (zooplankton) is greater than phytoplankton (the producer) because of their small size and low weight. The predatory fish is much larger than zooplankton.
- The producers of ocean or aquatic ecosystem are phytoplankton and they have short life span and turn over and have lower mass than zooplankton.
- The biomass of tropic level is depends on the longevity of member.

(d) Gases, responsible for Green house effect are carbon dioxide and methane.
A green house gas is a gas that absorbs and emits radiant energy within the thermal infrared range. Greenhouse gases cause the greenhouse effect. The primary greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are water vapour, carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide and ozone. Without greenhouse gases, the average temperature of Earth's surface would be about " <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>18</mn><msup><mrow /><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>, rather than the present average of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mn>15</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>59</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q1
rows.question: What is food web?
rows.answer: A food web is the natural interconnection of food chains and a graphical representation (usually an image) of what-eats-what) in an ecological community.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q2
rows.question: What are zooplanktons?
rows.answer: Zooplanktons are free floating small animals which usually feed on phytoplanktons, e.g., Daphnia, Paramaecium.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Define trophic level.
rows.answer: The trophic level of an organism is the position it occupies in a food chain.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q4
rows.question: What is biomass?
rows.answer: Biomass is amount of organic matter present in organisms.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q5
rows.question: What is ozone depletion ?
rows.answer: It is the reduction in the concentration of ozone in the ozone layer or ozonosphere.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q6
rows.question: What is ten percent law ?
rows.answer: Ten percent law is an enunciation given by Raymond Lindeman (1942) which states that only <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> of energy contained in a lower trophic level is trapped by next higher tropic level, the remaining <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>90</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> being lost in transfer and respiration of the latter.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q7
rows.question: Define ecological pyramid.
rows.answer: Ecological pyramid is a graphic representation of an ecological parameter (like biomass) sequence wise with producers at the base, herbivores above them followed by carnivores, secondary carnivores, etc. with the tip occupied by top carnivores.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q8
rows.question: What are secondary consumers?
rows.answer: Primary carnivores are called secondary consumers because they feed on first order or primary consumers (herbivores).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q9
rows.question: What is biological magnification?
rows.answer: Biological magnification (= biomagnification) is the increase in concentration of a chemical per unit weight of the organisms with successive rise in trophic level.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q10
rows.question: What is ozone? Where is ozone layer located?
rows.answer: Ozone is a molecule made of three atoms of oxygen, O3. Ozone layer is located at a height of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>11</mn><mo>−</mo><mn>16</mn><mrow><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow></mrow></math> above poles and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>23</mn><mo>−</mo><mn>25</mn><mrow><mtext> </mtext><mi mathvariant="normal">k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></mrow></mrow></math> above 'equator in stratosphere.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q11
rows.question: Name two non-biodegradable pollutants.
rows.answer: Plastics and pesticides.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q12
rows.question: Give one example of natural ecosystem and one example of human made artificial ecosystem.
rows.answer:
Natural ecosystem: Forests
Man-made ecosystem: Gardens

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q13
rows.question: What is meant by producers in an ecosystem?
rows.answer: Producers are those living organisms which can produce their own food by the process of photosynthesis (green plants).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q14
rows.question: Name three biotic components of an ecosystem.
rows.answer: Micro-organisms, animals and plants.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q15
rows.question: What is meant by consumers in an ecosystem?
rows.answer: Those organisms which cannot manufacture their food by the process of photosynthesis and depend either on plants or other organisms are known as consumers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q16
rows.question: Name any two abiotic components of an ecosystem.
rows.answer: Soil, water/air.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q17
rows.question: What are the two components of ecosystem?
rows.answer:
The two components of ecosystem are:
(i) Biotic components (all living organisms).
(ii) Abiotic components all (non-living components).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q18

rows.answer: Hydrofluorocarbon (HFC) and hydrochlorofluorocarbon (HCFC).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q19
rows.question: Write the names of aquatic organisms in order to form a food chain of at least three steps.
rows.answer: The names of aquatic organisms in order to form food chain is given below. Algae → insect → small fish → big fish.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q20

rows.answer:
UNEP: United Nations Environment Programme.
CFC: Chlorofluorocarbons.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q21
rows.question: How carbon monoxide is produced in the air?
rows.answer: Due to incomplete combustion of fossil fuels carbon monoxide is produced in the air.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q22
rows.question: Prepare a list of biotic and abiotic components of ecosystem.
rows.answer:
Biotic Components: Producers, consumers and decomposers.
Abiotic Components. Climatic factors (light, temperature, humidity, rainfall, air currents), edaphic factors (soil), topographic factors (surface of earth), inorganic and organic substances.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q23
rows.question: How can ultraviolet radiation affect the human body?
rows.answer: UV-radiations are very harmful as it causes skin cancer in human beings.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q24
rows.question: What is the role of biogeochemical cycles in the environment?
rows.answer: In biogeochemical cycles, essential nutrients like nitrogen, carbon, oxygen and water are changed from one form to another.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q25
rows.question: Write two natural sources of air pollution.
rows.answer: Two natural source of air pollution are forest fire and volcanic eruptions.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q26
rows.question: What are the pathways of energy and nutrients ?
rows.answer: Ecosystems are sustained by a continuous flow of energy from sunlight and a constant recycling of nutrients.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q27
rows.question: What is the essential role of decomposers in ecosystems?
rows.answer: Decomposers are essential because they recycle nutrients from dead organisms to the physical environment, where the nutrients may again be used by another organism.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q28
rows.question: Write two main sources of water pollution.
rows.answer:
The two main sources of water pollution are :
(i) Industries (ii) Sewage

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q29
rows.question: Why are plants called transducers or converters?
rows.answer: Plants are called transducers or converters because they convert light energy into chemical energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q30
rows.question: What happens when in a food-chain tertiary consumers are destroyed ?
rows.answer: When tertiary consumers are destroyed in a food chain then increase in number of secondary consumers is observed.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Very Short Answer Questions: — Q31
rows.question: What is the achievement of UNEP convention of 1987 ?
rows.answer: UNEP convention of 1987 succeeded in forging agreement for reduction in the production of chlorofluorocarbons to half the level of 1986.

rows.answer: (a) Lichens produce small amounts of carbonic acids from their "roots" and these slowly dissolve the rock, releasing nutrients. Other nutrients are obtained from water-borne or air-borne particles of both organic and inorganic materials. The body of a lichen contains an algae able to photosynthesise sugars, some of which are passed on to the fungal component of the lichen. In return the algae gets a tiny but significant amount of shelter within the tissues of the lichen.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Long Answer Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Describe a grassland food chain.
rows.answer:
A food chain in a grassland ecosystem may consist of grasses and other plants, grasshoppers, frogs, snakes and hawks. A food chain always begins with producers. Herbivores (plant-eaters) come next in the chain. They are consumed by carnivores (flesh-eaters).
(I) Producers: The primary producers of food are the grasses. If herbs and shrubs are present, they also contribute to the primary production of food.
(II) Consumers: The consumers in a grassland ecosystem are of three levels.
(a) Primary consumers: These feed directly from the grasses (grazing) and include herbivores such as Cows, Buffaloes, Goats, Rabbits, Mouse etc. and also insects, termites, centipede, millipedes etc.
(b) Secondary consumers: These consumers are the carnivorous animals such as snakes, lizard, jackal, foxes, frogs etc. which feed on the primary consumers.
(c) Tertiary consumers: Hawk, Eagles and vultures constitute the tertiary consumer in the grassland ecosystem which preys upon the secondary and primary consumer.
(III) Decomposers: The organic matter of the grassland is decomposed by the microbes like actinomycetes, fungi (Mucor, Aspergillus, Rhizopus, Penicillium, and Cladosporium), aerobic and anaerobic soil bacteria etc. They release the minerals back into the soil thus making the soil fertile.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Long Answer Questions: — Q2
rows.question: Explain some harmful effects of agricultural practices on the environment.
rows.answer:
(i) Soil - Fertilizer added to soil not only changes the chemistry of the soil but also kills many useful microbes.
(ii) Ground Water - A part of fertilizer always leaches down into soil and reaches ground water. It raises the salt content of ground water.
(iii) Eutrophication - Run-off from fields sprayed with fertilizer reaches water bodies. It results in their eutrophication.
(iv) Pesticides - Pesticides sprayed over crops reach water bodies killing the biota. Persistent pesticides undergo biomagnification and prove highly harmful to higher organisms.
(vi) Ground Water - Continued use of ground water in agriculture has resulted in lowering of water table at most of the places.
(vii) Irrigation - It causes water-logging and salivation of soils.
(viii) Genetic Erosion - Use of only selected high yielding varieties has resulted in genetic erosion of the crop plants.
(ix) Damage to Nature - Natural ecosystems and habitats have been damaged during clearing land for agriculture.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Long Answer Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Suggest suitable mechanisms for waste management in fertilizer industries.
rows.answer:
Fertilizer industries produce mainly two types of wastes
(a) Gaseous (b) Effluents. Additionally they may release heat and fuel waste if coal is being used as a source of energy.
(a) Gaseous wastes - They are passed through wet scrubbers to dissolve the pollutant gases.
(b) Effluents - The effluents of the industry are tested for chemicals present in them. The same can be
retrieved and made available to the market. Hot effluents are cooled. Acidic or alkaline nature can be corrected. Heavy metals and toxins can be separated. Only treated and harmless effluents are allowed to be discharged into surrounding environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Long Answer Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Explain the flow of energy between various components of the environment.
rows.answer:
(i) Green plants in a terrestrial ecosystem capture about <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>1</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> of the energy of sunlight that falls on their leaves.
(ii) When herbivore (primary consumers) eat plants a great deal of energy is lost as heat to the environment, some amount is used for various life processes. An average of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> of the food eaten is turned into its own body and made available for the next level of consumers.
(iii) The loss of energy at each step is so great that very little usable energy remains after four trophic levels.
(iv) In a food chain the greatest number of individuals are at lower trophic level of an ecosystem. The greatest number is of the producers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Explain why, it is better to use paper bags than plastic bags.
rows.answer: It's better to use paper bags than plastic bags because paper is recyclable whereas plastic is not. Plastic cannot be degraded and they pollute the environment; whereas paper is biodegradable and does not cause any pollution. Moreover, even if burned, paper will not cause much pollution but plastics on burning will release toxic chemicals into the environment thus polluting the environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q2
rows.question: Why are community interactions important?
rows.answer: Community interactions influence population size and the interacting populations with communities act on one another as agents of natural selection. Thus, community interactions also shape the bodies and behaviors of the interacting populations.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q3
rows.question: How do kulhads (claycups) affect the soil fertility?
rows.answer: Kulhdas or clay cups are made from the soil or clay that is used for agriculture and is very fertile. So if these cups are made in large scales then the fertility of the soil decreases and renders the land unfertile and unfit for cultivation.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q4
rows.question: Why are there rarely more than five links (or five organisms) in a food chain?
rows.answer: There are rarely more than five links (five organisms) in a food chain because there is a decrease in energy with every transfer to next trophic level and energy availability will be so small that animals will not be able to survive with small amount of energy.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q5
rows.question: Indicate the flow of energy in an ecosystem. Why is it undirectional? Justify.
rows.answer:
An ecosystem does not have its own source of energy. It receives the same from sun. Green plants or producers trap the solar energy and change it into chemical form during synthesis of food. Herbivores obtain energy from the food they take. A lot of energy dissipates during transfer and utilization of food energy by herbivores
( <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> law). From herbivores the food energy passes to primary carnivores. However, only about <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> of herbivore energy is passed into body mass of primary carnivores. The rest is dissipated. From primary carnivores the energy passes into secondary carnivores ( <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> ), etc. It is ultimately lost as heat.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q6
rows.question: We do not clean ponds or lakes but an aquarium needs to be cleaned. Why?
rows.answer: An aquarium is an artificial system which is also incomplete due to absence of producers, food chains and decomposers. There is no recycling and self-cleaning. However, a pond or a lake is a self-sustained, natural and complete ecosystem where there is perfect recycling of nutrients.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Reasoning Based Questions: — Q7
rows.question: 'If we excessively use pesticides to protect the crops from diseases, then it may cause long-term damage to mankind'. Justify this statement.
rows.answer: Pesticides are non- biodegradable chemicals, so they get accumulated in each tropic. Since human occupy the top position in each tropic level, so the maximum amount of harmful chemical pesticides get accumulated in our bodies, which damages our health gradually.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q1
rows.question: What would happen if all the decomposers were eliminated from the earth? Explain.
rows.answer: Elimination of decomposers will cause (i) Piling up of excretions, litter and dead bodies of plants and animals. Soon there will be no free space for new generations of organisms. (ii) Non-cycling of biogenetic nutrients. As a result all the available biogenetic nutrients will be tied up in organic matter. Further synthesis of organic matter will stop resulting in destruction of all life.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q2
rows.question: What is the impact of garbage on the environemnt?
rows.answer: A good amount of garbage is generated each day in the cities which may consist of glass bottles, crockery, plastic containers, polythene and other packing materials, automobile spares, machines, cycle parts, construction wastes, sludge, dead animal skeletons, crop residues, etc. These clog drains, do not look aesthetic, may be eaten mistakenly by animals, and pile up at public places and obstruct daily life. Heavy metals from garbage may leach into the soil and cause ground water pollution. Garbage may stink giving bad odour and fumes all around the area. Most of the garbage is non-biodegradable and causes immense harm to the environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — HOTS Questions: — Q3
rows.question: "Damage to the ozone layer is a cause of concern." Justify the statement. Suggest any two steps to limit this damage.
rows.answer: Cause of Concern: Ozone layer present in the stratosphere has thinned out by about <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>8</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> over the equator and more so over the antarctica where a big ozone hole appears every year. This has increased the level of UV-B radiations reaching the earth by 15 <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>20</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>. These radiations are causing increased number of skin cancers, cataracts and reduced immunity in human beings. There is increased incidence of blinding of animals, death of young ones, reduced photosynthesis, higher number of mutations and damage to organisms.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q1
rows.question: Why are some substances biodegradable and some nonbiodegradable?
rows.answer: The micro-organisms like bacteria and other decomposer organisms (called saprophytes) present in our environment are 'specific' in their action. They break down the natural materials or products made from natural materials (say, paper) but do not break down man-made materials such as plastics. So, it is due to the property of decomposer organisms of being specific in their action that some waste materials are biodegradable whereas others are non-biodegradable.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q2
rows.question: Give any two ways in which biodegradable substances would affect the environment.
rows.answer:
The two ways in which biodegradable substances would affect the environment are:
(i) Biodegradable wastes usually do not pollute the environment. It pollutes the environment only when their amount is large and cannot be degraded (or decomposed) into harmless substances in nature at the right time.
(ii) The biodegradable substance mainly contains carbon. These substances after decomposition release that carbon back to the atmosphere.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q3
rows.question: Give any two ways in which non-biodegradable sustances would affect the environment.
rows.answer:
The two ways in which non-biodegradable substances affect the environment are:
(i) Non-biodegradable wastes are the major pollutants of the environment. For example, the discarded plastic articles, glass articles and metal objects are the non-biodegradable waste materials which cause a lot of pollution in our surroundings.
(ii) Use of non-biodegradable substances such as polythene results in blocking of drainage system and also harms animals when they are eaten by animals.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q4
rows.question: What are trophic levels? Give an example of a food chain and state the different trophic levels in it.
rows.answer: The various steps in a food chain at which the transfer of food (or energy) takes place are called trophic levels. The producers form the first trophic level as they manufacture food. The primary consumers form the second trophic level, the secondary consumers form the third, and the tertiary consumers form the fourth trophic level.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Questions: — Q5
rows.question: What is the role of decomposers in the ecosystem?

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q1
rows.question:
Which of the following groups contain only biodegradable items ?
(a) Grass, flowers and leather
(b) Grass, wood and plastic
(c) Fruit-peels, cake and lime-juice
(d) Cake, wood and grass
rows.answer:
(c) and (d) The term biodegradable is used to describe materials that decompose through the actions of bacteria, fungi, and other living organisms. Temperature and sunlight may also play roles in the decomposition of biodegradable plastics and other substances.
Example: human and animal excreta, plant products like rubber, paper, wood, leaves, cotton, and wool, dead remains of living organisms, kitchen waste, agricultural waste.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q2
rows.question:
Which of the following constitute a food-chain?
(a) Grass, wheat and mango
(b) Grass, goat and human
(c) Goat, cow and elephant
(d) Grass, fish and goat
rows.answer: (b) Food chain is a linear sequence of organisms which starts from producer organism and ends with decomposer species. In the given group, grass is a producer, goat is a herbivore and human is top carnivore.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q3
rows.question:
Which of the following are environment-friendly practices?
(a) Carrying cloth-bags to put purchases in while shopping.
(b) Switching off unnecessary lights and fans.
(c) Walking to school instead of getting your mother to drop you on her scooter.
(d) All of the above.
rows.answer: (d) Eco-friendly products promote green living that helps to conserve energy and also prevent air, water and noise pollution. They prove to be a boon for the environment and also prevent human health from deterioration.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q4
rows.question: What will happen if we kill all the organisms in one trophic level?
rows.answer: If we kill all the organisms in one trophic level, then it will disturb the entire ecosystem. The transfer of food energy to the next trophic level will stop. This will cause a break in the food chain. As a result, the organisms of the higher trophic level will also die, while the individuals of the lower trophic level will exhibit enormous growth in their population.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q5
rows.question: Will the impact of removing all the organisms in a trophic level be different for different trophic levels? Can the organisms of any trophic level be removed without causing any damage to the ecosystem?
rows.answer:
Yes, removing all the organisms in a tropic level is different for different tropic levels. If the organisms of any trophic level be removed it will certainly damage the ecosystem. For example, if all the producers are killed, it will cause death or migration of primary consumers in an ecosystem. But if primary consumers are removed, it will lead to the cause of death of higher trophic level, while those are of lower level (producers) will show an exponential growth.
The survival of organism of one trophic level depends upon the existence of the member of second trophic level. All the categories of organisms are linked with food chain, so removal of the organisms in a trophic level will disturb the whole ecosystem.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q6
rows.question: What is biological magnification? Will the levels of this magnification be different at different levels of the ecosystem?
rows.answer:
The increase in concentration of harmful chemical substances like pesticides in the body of living organisms at each trophic level of a food chain is called biological magnification.
Yes, magnification will be different at different trophic levels. The level of these harmful substances will go on increasing from one trophic level to the next because when certain harmful substances enter the food chain at the level of primary producers, they get concentrated many times at each subsequent trophic level.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q7
rows.question: What are the problems caused by the non-biodegradable wastes that we generate?
rows.answer:
The problems caused by the non-biodegradable wastes that we generate are as follows:
(i) Non-biodegradable wastes persist in the environment for a long time and cause greater harm to the various members of the ecosystem by causing biological magnification.
(ii) Some of the non-biodegradable waste (like metals, DDT, plastics) enter the food chain of the human and damage their health for long run.
(iii) Non-biodegradable waste such as fertilisers, pesticides, weedicides, etc., changes the soil chemistry which affects the fertility of soil and subsequently reduces the crop yield.
(iv) Burning non-biodegradable wastes can lead to produce harmful gases in the environment causing air pollution.
(v) It leads to the destruction of balance of ecological environment.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Text - Book Exercise: — Q8
rows.question: If all the waste we generate is biodegradable, will this have no impact on the environment?
rows.answer:
If all the waste we generate is biodegradable then it will have an adverse effect on the environment. It affects the environment in the following ways:
(i) Too much of biodegradable waste will not be broken down into simpler substances by decomposers at the right time, which will go on accumulating in the environment and act as a pollutant.
(ii) Slow decomposition of biodegradable waste leads to the release of foul smell and harmful gases. When these gases inhaled by human beings, they may cause nausea, irritation, etc.
(iii) Increase in the population of micro-organism in an aquatic medium, leads to the deficiency of oxygen in water bodies.
(iv) Blockage of drains due to the accumulation of large amount of bio-degradable waste also causes unhygienic conditions and multiplication of mosquitoes, which spread diseases like malaria and dengue.

rows.answer: (a) Lichens produce small amounts of carbonic acids from their "roots" and these slowly dissolve the rock, releasing nutrients. In return the algae gets a tiny but significant amount of shelter within the tissues of the lichen.

(d) Ecological pyramid is a graphical representation that illustrates the flow of energy through different forms of life in an ecosystem. It is generally inverted in case of pyramid of biomass in a sea because the biomass of the fishes is more than the phytoplanktons (producers).
Features of pyramid of biomass in a sea:
- Food chain in ocean - Phytoplankton → zooplankton → predatory fish
- The biomass of consumers (zooplankton) is greater than phytoplankton (the producer) because of their small size and low weight. The predatory fish is much larger than zooplankton.
- The producers of ocean or aquatic ecosystem are phytoplankton and they have short life span and turn over and have lower mass than zooplankton.
- The biomass of tropic level is depends on the longevity of member.

(d) Gases, responsible for Green house effect are carbon dioxide and methane.
A green house gas is a gas that absorbs and emits radiant energy within the thermal infrared range. Greenhouse gases cause the greenhouse effect. The primary greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are water vapour, carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide and ozone. Without greenhouse gases, the average temperature of Earth's surface would be about " <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>18</mn><msup><mrow /><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>, rather than the present average of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mn>15</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>59</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>.

(a) Montreal protocol - Signed in 16 Sep, 1987 (Ozone day) came into force - 1 Jan, 1989. It was aimed at stopping the production and import of ODS and reduce their concentration in the atmosphere.
The main uses of ozone depleting substances include:
- CFCs and HCFCs in refrigerators and air conditioners,
- HCFCs and halons in fire extinguishers,

(iv) Length of food chain may vary from 2-3 trophic levels.
(a) (i) and (iv)
(b) (i) and (ii)
(c) (ii) and (iii)
(d) (iii) and (iv)
rows.answer:
Which two of the following statements regarding food chains are correct?
(i) Removal of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>80</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> tigers resulted in increased growth of vegetation.
(ii) Removal of most carnivores resulted in increased population of deer.
(iii) Length of food chain is limited to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>−</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></math> trophic levels due to energy loss.
(iv) Length of food chain may vary from 2-3 trophic levels.
(a) (i) and (iv)
(b) (i) and (ii)
(c) (ii) and (iii)
(d) (iii) and (iv)

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q1
rows.question:
In an ecosystem the function of the producers is:
(a) To convert organic compound into inorganic form
(b) To utilise chemical energy
(c) To trap solar energy and convert into chemical energy
(d) To release energy

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q2
rows.question:
Which of the following food chain produces a greater availablity of food to next trophic level?
(a) Diatoms → Fish → Large fish → Birds → Man
(b) Green plant → Rabbit → Man
(c) Green plant → Grasshopper → Frog → Snake → Peacock
(d) Green plant → Rabbit → wolf → Man
rows.answer: (b) Shorter the food chain, more is the availability of food to consumer.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q3
rows.question:
If a big fish eats small fish which eats Hydra which in turn eats water fleas. Water fleas in turn eat phytoplanktons. In this chain, water fleas will be:
(a) Producers
(b) Primary consumers
(c) Secondary consumers
(d) Top consumer

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q4
rows.question:
Which of the following pyramids represents the variation in biomass at different trophic levels in pond ecosystem?
(a)
(b)
(c)
(d)
rows.answer: (b) Whale is carnivorous and feeds on primary consumer and occupies the third trophic level of the ecosystem.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q5
rows.question:
Which of the following statement is incorrect about energy flow in an ecosystem?
(a) Pyramid of energy is always straight.
(b) Energy flow is unidirectional.
(c) Energy flow is cyclic.
(d) Ecological efficiency is generally <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>10</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math>.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q6
rows.question:
Whale is:
(a) Primary producer
(b) Carnivorous, secondary consumer
(c) A decomposer
(d) Herbivorous
rows.answer: (d) The greenhouse effect is a process by which thermal radiation from a planetary surface is absorbed by atmospheric greenhouse gases, and is re-radiated in all directions. Re-designing landfill dumps to allow methane to be collected slows down the green house effect.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q7
rows.question:
Which of the following strategy is an incorrect approach to reduce global warming?
(a) Reducing the greenhouse gas emission by limiting the use of fossil fuels.
(b) Increase the vegetation cover particularly the forest for photosynthetic utilisation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math>.
(c) Minimising the use of nitrogen fertilisers in agriculture for reducing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mi>N</mi><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi>O</mi></mrow></math> emission.
(d) Increasing the use of air conditioners, refrigeration unit and production of plastic foams and propellants in aerosal spray cans.
rows.answer: (d) Global warming is increase of earth's average temperature due to greenhouse effect which is naturally occurring phenomenon that is responsible for heating of Earth's surface and atmosphere. The main greenhouse gas is carbon-dioxide (contribute <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>60</mn><mi>%</mi></mrow></math> ). In first three options, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></math> concentration increases but it decreases in option (d).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q8
rows.question:
Today the concentration of greenhouse gases is very high because of:
(a) Use of refrigerator
(b) Increased combustion of oils and coal
(c) Deforestation
(d) All of the above
rows.answer: (d) The gradual and predictable change, in the composition of species takes place in a given area during ecological succession.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q9
rows.question:
Which of the following would be most likely to help to slow down the greenhouse effect?
(a) Ensuring that all excess paper packaging is burned to ashes.
(b) Promoting the use of private rather than public transport.
(c) Converting tropical forests into grazing land for cattle.
(d) Re-designing land-fill dumps to allow methane to be collected.
rows.answer: (a) Biotic community is an association of individuals of different species living in the same habitat and showing functional interactions.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q10
rows.question:
Global warming can be controlled by:
(a) reducing reforestation, increasing the use of fossil fuel.
(b) increasing deforestation, slowing down the growth of human population.
(c) increasing deforestation, reducing efficiency of energy usage.
(d) reducing deforestation, cutting down use of fossil fuel.
rows.answer: (d) Biomagnification means an increase in concentration of toxins through the trophic levels of a food chain.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q11
rows.question:
During ecological succession:
[JSTSE]
(a) the establishment of a new biotic community is very fast in its primary phase.
(b) the numbers and types of animals remain constant.
(c) the changes lead to a community that is in near equilibrium with the environment and is called pioneer community.
(d) the gradual and predictable change in species composition occurs in a given area.
rows.answer: (a) Lichens produce small amounts of carbonic acids from their "roots" and these slowly dissolve the rock, releasing nutrients. Other nutrients are obtained from water-borne or air-borne particles of both organic and inorganic materials. The body of a lichen contains an algae able to photosynthesise sugars, some of which are passed on to the fungal component of the lichen. In return the algae gets a tiny but significant amount of shelter within the tissues of the lichen.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q12
rows.question:
An association of individuals of different species living in the same habitat and having functional interactions is:
[JSTSE]
(a) Biotic community
(b) Ecosystem
(c) Population
(d) Ecological niche
rows.answer: (b) Sir Arthur George Tansley was an English botanist and a pioneer in the science of ecology who coined the term ecosystem.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q13
rows.question:
Increase in concentration of the toxicant at successive trophic levels is known as:
[JSTSE]
(a) Biodeterioration
(b) Biotransformation
(c) Biogeochemical
(d) Biomagnification
rows.answer: (b) Cropland ecosystem is largest anthropogenic ecosystem characterised by less diversity and high productivity.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q14
rows.question:

[JSTSE]
(a) Lichens
(b) Liverworts
(c) Mosses
(d) Green algae
rows.answer:
(d) Forest ecosystem has the maximum biomass because it includes organisms of all tropic levels. Some very highly productive ecosystems are:
- Tropical rain forest
- Coral reef
- Estuaries
- Sugarcane fields

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q15
rows.question:
The term ecosystem was coined by:
[JSTSE]
(a) E.P. Odum
(b) A.G. Tansley
(c) E.Haeckel
(d) E. Warming
rows.answer: Those organisms which cannot manufacture their food by the process of photosynthesis and depend either on plants or other organisms are known as consumers.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q16
rows.question:

[JSTSE]
(a) Absence of soil organisms
(b) Least genetic diversity
(c) Absence of weeds
(d) Ecological succession
rows.answer: Soil, water/air.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q17
rows.question:
Which ecosystem has the maximum biomass?
[JSTSE]
(a) Grassland ecosystem
(b) Pond ecosystem
(c) Lake ecosystem
(d) Forest ecosystem
rows.answer:
The two components of ecosystem are:
(i) Biotic components (all living organisms).
(ii) Abiotic components all (non-living components).

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q18
rows.question:
What type of ecological pyramid would be obtained with the following data?
[JSTSE]
Secondary consumer : 120 g
Primary consumer : 60 g
Primary producer : 10 g
(a) Inverted pyramid of biomass
(b) Pyramid of energy
(c) Upright pyramid of biomass
(d) Upright pyramid of numbers
rows.answer: (a) The given data depicts the inverted pyramid of biomass, usually present in aquatic ecosystem. Upright pyramid of biomass and numbers are not possible, as the data depicts primary producer is less than primary consumer and this is less than secondary consumers. Pyramid of energy is always upright.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q19
rows.question:
Which one of the following substance is non-biodegradable?
[JSTSE]
(a) Paper
(b) Manure
(c) Cotton cloth
(d) DDT

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q20
rows.question:
Which of the following ecological pyramids is generally inverted?
[JSTSE]
(a) Pyramid of numbers in grassland
(b) Pyramid of energy
(c) Pyramid of biomass in a forest
(d) Pyramid of biomass in a sea
rows.answer:
(d) Ecological pyramid is a graphical representation that illustrates the flow of energy through different forms of life in an ecosystem. It is generally inverted in case of pyramid of biomass in a sea because the biomass of the fishes is more than the phytoplanktons (producers).
Features of pyramid of biomass in a sea:
- Food chain in ocean - Phytoplankton → zooplankton → predatory fish
- The biomass of consumers (zooplankton) is greater than phytoplankton (the producer) because of their small size and low weight. The predatory fish is much larger than zooplankton.
- The producers of ocean or aquatic ecosystem are phytoplankton and they have short life span and turn over and have lower mass than zooplankton.
- The biomass of tropic level is depends on the longevity of member.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q21
rows.question:
Which of the following pairs of gases is mainly responsible for green house effect?
[JSTSE]
(a) Ozone and Ammonia
(b) Oxygen and Nitrogen
(c) Nitrogen and Sulphur dioxide
(d) Carbon dioxide and Methane
rows.answer:
(d) Gases, responsible for Green house effect are carbon dioxide and methane.
A green house gas is a gas that absorbs and emits radiant energy within the thermal infrared range. Greenhouse gases cause the greenhouse effect. The primary greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are water vapour, carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide and ozone. Without greenhouse gases, the average temperature of Earth's surface would be about " <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><mn>18</mn><msup><mrow /><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>0</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>, rather than the present average of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" display="inline"><mrow><msup><mn>15</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>C</mi><mrow><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="prefix">(</mo><msup><mn>59</mn><mrow><mo>∘</mo></mrow></msup><mi>F</mi><mo stretchy="true" fence="true" form="postfix">)</mo></mrow></mrow></math>.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q22
rows.question:
Montreal protocol was signed in 1987 for control of
[JSTSE]
(a) Emission of ozone depleting substances
(b) Release of Green House gases
(c) Disposal of e-wastes
(d) Transport of Genetically modified organisms from one country to another
rows.answer:
(a) Montreal protocol - Signed in 16 Sep, 1987 (Ozone day) came into force - 1 Jan, 1989. It was aimed at stopping the production and import of ODS and reduce their concentration in the atmosphere.
The main uses of ozone depleting substances include:
- CFCs and HCFCs in refrigerators and air conditioners,
- HCFCs and halons in fire extinguishers,
- CFCs and HCFCs in foam,
- CFCs and HCFCs as aerosol propellants, and
- Methyl bromide for fumigation of soil, structures and goods to be imported or exported.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q23
rows.question:
Snow-blindness in Antarctic region is due to
[JSTSE]
(a) Inflammation of cornea due to high dose of UV-B radiation
(b) High reflection of light from snow
(c) Damage to retina caused by infra-red rays
(d) Freezing of fluids in the eye by low temperature
rows.answer: (a) UV-B radiations damage DNA and mutations may occur. In human eye, cornea absorbs UV-B radiations, and a high dose of UV-B causes inflammation of cornea called snow blindness, cataract, etc.

rows.title: Passage Based Questions: — Directions — Q24
rows.question:
In water hyacinth and water lily, pollination takes place by :
[JSTSE]
(a) Water currents only
(b) Wind and water
(c) Insects and water
(d) Insects or wind
rows.answer: (d) In majority of aquatic plants, the flowers emerge above the level of water. These may be pollinated by insects or wind eg.: Water hyacinth and water lily.
